news.title: T-Mobile introduces its Perplexity AI-powered T Phone 3 and T Tablet 2
news.summary: Deutsche Telekom, T-Mobile's parent company, has announced the T Phone 3 and T Tablet 2, both featuring Perplexity AI for task accomplishment without traditional apps.
news.content: The T Phone 3 comes with a 6.58-inch LTPS LCD and is equipped with Qualcomm’s Snapdragon 6 Gen 3 chipset. It also includes a 5,000 mAh battery with 25W charging and IP54 ingress protection. The device runs Android 15 with Magenta AI on top and offers 3 Android upgrades and 6 years of software support. The Perplexity AI assistant allows users to perform tasks via voice commands or camera searches, aiming to replace the need to switch between apps.

news.title: Nothing Phone 3 rumored to include Snapdragon 8s Gen 3 chip
news.summary: The upcoming Nothing Phone 3 is expected to feature the Snapdragon 8s Gen 3 chip, offering advanced AI features such as on-device generative AI.
news.content: The Snapdragon 8s Gen 3 chip is designed for affordable flagship phones and shares technology with the premium Snapdragon 8 Gen 3. It offers slightly less power but includes features like on-device generative AI and intelligent photography tools. This could significantly boost the Nothing Phone 3's performance compared to its predecessors and potentially improve battery life due to the more efficient 4-nanometer technology.

news.title: Meta rolls out prescription-optimized Ray-Ban Meta glasses
news.summary: Meta introduces new Ray-Ban Meta glasses designed to support nearly all prescriptions, catering to those who rely on prescription glasses.
news.content: The new Ray-Ban Meta glasses are built to better serve people who rely on prescription glasses and all-day eyewear. They feature overextension hinges, interchangeable nose pads, and optician-adjustable temple tips for a tailored fit. The glasses also come with advanced software updates like hands-free nutrition tracking and Meta AI WhatsApp summaries.

news.title: Google acquires smart glasses maker North
news.summary: Google has acquired North, the company behind the Focals smart glasses, signaling Google's continued investment in smart glasses technology.
news.content: North, a Canadian company known for its Focals smart glasses, has been acquired by Google. This move indicates Google's ongoing commitment to developing ambient computing future through smart glasses. The acquisition also implies that North's technical expertise will aid Google's hardware efforts.

news.title: MediaTek Dimensity 9500 brings low-power AI camera upgrades
news.summary: MediaTek's new Dimensity 9500 chip for smartphones offers significant improvements in AI processing, imaging, and power efficiency.
news.content: The Dimensity 9500 is MediaTek's next-gen flagship chip expected to power many top Android phones in the coming year. It features a third-generation All Big Core CPU for higher performance and power efficiency. The new Super Efficient NPU tech will reduce power consumption, enabling AI models to run continuously. The chip also supports 200MP cameras and 4K, 60fps portrait video.

news.title: Deutsche Telekom's AI Phone launching later this year
news.summary: Deutsche Telekom confirms the official launch of its AI Phone in the second half of 2025, powered by Perplexity AI.
news.content: The AI Phone will feature a custom Magenta AI operating system which replaces the traditional app-based user interface with Perplexity Assistant. The device relies on multimodal input, allowing users to perform tasks via voice, typing, or camera searches. Deutsche Telekom plans to roll out the device to key European markets in 2026.

news.title: Smart glasses are forcing wearables to get very weird
news.summary: The advent of smart glasses has led to the development of unique wearables like Meta's Neural Band, an EMG wristband that controls smart glasses.
news.content: Smart glasses like Meta's Ray-Ban Display are being accompanied by innovative wearables such as the Neural Band, which reads electrical signals from the user's arm and fingers to control the glasses. This development signifies a new direction for wearables, focusing on providing intuitive controls for smart glasses.

news.title: Rokid launches new AR glasses for enterprise use
news.summary: Rokid, a leading AR glasses manufacturer, has announced a new model designed for enterprise applications, focusing on productivity and collaboration.
news.content: Rokid's new AR glasses are tailored for business use, offering features that enhance工作效率 and team collaboration. The glasses provide hands-free operation and real-time data visualization, which are crucial for industries such as manufacturing, logistics, and field services.

news.title: Huawei unveils MateView AI, an intelligent monitor
news.summary: Huawei has introduced the MateView AI, a monitor that integrates AI capabilities for smart task management and enhanced user interaction.
news.content: The MateView AI monitor from Huawei features built-in AI that can recognize user behavior and adjust settings automatically. It also supports voice commands and can connect with other Huawei devices for a seamless workflow.

news.title: Zephyr RTOS 2.4 release focuses on security
news.summary: The latest version of the Zephyr RTOS, 2.4, includes significant enhancements to security features, catering to the needs of IoT devices.
news.content: Zephyr RTOS 2.4 introduces a range of security improvements, including encrypted storage, secure boot, and runtime protection. These features are designed to protect IoT devices from potential threats and ensure data integrity.

news.title: FreeRTOS announces new partner program
news.summary: FreeRTOS has launched a new partner program to support developers in creating secure and reliable IoT solutions.
news.content: The FreeRTOS partner program provides access to training, resources, and community support for developers working on IoT projects. This initiative aims to foster innovation and improve the security of IoT devices built on the FreeRTOS platform.

news.title: Tuya Smart launches new AI platform for home automation
news.summary: Tuya Smart has introduced a new AI platform designed to simplify home automation and enhance user experience.
news.content: The new AI platform from Tuya Smart offers a range of APIs and tools for developers to create smart home solutions. It includes features like voice control, facial recognition, and automated scene creation, making it easier for users to manage their smart homes.

news.title: Baidu's AI cloud service expands in Europe
news.summary: Baidu has announced the expansion of its AI cloud services into Europe, offering advanced AI capabilities to local businesses.
news.content: Baidu's AI cloud service, which includes machine learning, data analytics, and AI development tools, is now available in Europe. This expansion allows European companies to access Baidu's advanced AI technologies and services, supporting their digital transformation.

news.title: New smart home security system integrates with major AI platforms
news.summary: A new smart home security system has been launched, offering compatibility with major AI platforms like Amazon Alexa and Google Assistant.
news.content: The new smart home security system allows users to control their home security through voice commands and AI-driven automation. It integrates seamlessly with popular AI platforms, providing a unified control experience for smart home management.

news.title: Researchers develop AI chip for edge devices
news.summary: A team of researchers has created an AI chip specifically designed for edge devices, enhancing processing capabilities and power efficiency.
news.content: The new AI chip is designed to perform complex AI tasks on edge devices without the need for constant cloud connectivity. It offers improved processing speed and power efficiency, making it suitable for IoT devices and wearables.
